metadata.title: Claude Code v2 — Complete System Internals
metadata.name: Claude CodeComplete Internals
metadata.description: Every detail of the system prompt, tools, agent types, skills, MCP servers, behavioral rules, memory system, and environment—extracted from a live Claude Code session.

tools.name: path
tools.description: File or directory to search in

tools.name: head_limit
tools.description: Limit output to first N entries

cards.title: Claude Code
cards.description: Anthropic's official CLI for Claude. An interactive agent for software engineering tasks—solving bugs, adding features, refactoring, explaining code, and more.

cards.title: Opus 4.6
cards.description: Model ID: claude-opus-4-6. Knowledge cutoff: May 2025. Part of the Claude 4.5/4.6 family. The most capable model available.

rows: Latest and most capable Claude models

content.html: Claude Code runs as the main agent, with the ability to spawn subagents (one-shot or persistent team members), access MCP servers, invoke skills, and manage persistent memory.
The system prompt is divided into multiple sections, each governing different behaviors. Here is every rule, verbatim intent preserved.
Measure twice, cut once. For reversible local actions (editing files, running tests), proceed freely. For hard-to-reverse or shared-system actions, confirm with the user first.
Every tool available in this Claude Code session. Grouped by category.

cards.title: Read
cards.description: Read files from the filesystem. Supports text, images (PNG, JPG), PDFs (with page ranges), Jupyter notebooks. Default: up to 2000 lines, truncation at 2000 chars/line. Cat -n format with line numbers starting at 1.

cards.title: Edit
cards.description: Exact string replacements in files. Requires Read first. Must preserve exact indentation. Fails if old_string is not unique. Use replace_all for renaming across a file.

cards.title: Write
cards.description: Write/overwrite files. Requires Read first for existing files. Prefers editing over creating new files. Never proactively creates docs/README files.

cards.title: NotebookEdit
cards.description: Replace, insert, or delete cells in Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb). 0-indexed cell numbers. Supports code and markdown cell types.

content.html: Critical rule: Always use dedicated file tools (Read, Edit, Write) instead of Bash with cat, sed, awk, echo, head, tail. Bash is reserved for system commands only.

cards.title: Glob
cards.description: Fast file pattern matching. Supports glob patterns (**/*.js, src/**/*.ts). Returns matching paths sorted by modification time. Use instead of find or ls.

cards.title: Grep
cards.description: Ripgrep-powered content search. Full regex, glob/type filters. Modes: content, files_with_matches (default), count. Supports multiline matching. Use instead of grep or rg.

rows: File or directory to search in
Limit output to first N entries

cards.title: Bash
cards.description: Execute bash commands with optional timeout (max 600s, default 120s). Working directory persists; shell state does not. Supports background execution. Directory verification required before creating files/dirs.

cards.title: Task
cards.description: Launch specialized subagents. Requires subagent_type. Can join teams with team_name. Supports run_in_background, resume by agent ID, and model selection (sonnet/opus/haiku).

cards.title: TeamCreate
cards.description: Create a team with shared task list. Creates ~/.claude/teams/{name}/ and ~/.claude/tasks/{name}/.

cards.title: TeamDelete
cards.description: Remove team and task directories. Fails if active members remain. Shutdown teammates first.

cards.title: SendMessage
cards.description: 5 types: message, broadcast, shutdown_request, shutdown_response, plan_approval_response. Only way agents communicate.

cards.title: AskUserQuestion
cards.description: Ask the user 1-4 questions with 2-4 options each. Users can always select "Other" for custom input. Supports multiSelect. Use for preferences, clarification, decisions.

cards.title: WebFetch
cards.description: Fetch and process URL content with AI. Converts HTML to markdown. 15-min cache. Fails for authenticated/private URLs. Auto-upgrades HTTP to HTTPS.

cards.title: WebSearch
cards.description: Real-time web search. Must include Sources section with URLs in response. Use current year (2026) in queries. Domain filtering supported.

cards.title: TaskCreate
cards.description: Create tasks with subject, description, activeForm. Use for 3+ step tasks. Tasks start as pending with no owner.

cards.title: TaskList
cards.description: List all tasks: id, subject, status, owner, blockedBy. Use to find available work or check progress.

cards.title: TaskGet
cards.description: Full task details by ID: description, dependencies, status, blocks, blockedBy.

cards.title: TaskUpdate
cards.description: Swiss army knife: set owner, status, dependencies (addBlocks/addBlockedBy), description, activeForm. Status: pending → in_progress → completed (or deleted).

cards.title: EnterPlanMode
cards.description: Transition to plan mode for non-trivial implementations. Explore codebase, design approach, get user approval before coding. Use for: new features, multiple approaches, multi-file changes, unclear requirements.

cards.title: ExitPlanMode
cards.description: Signal plan completion for user review. Only for implementation planning (not research). Plan must be written to plan file first.

cards.title: Skill
cards.description: Invoke user-configurable skills (slash commands like /commit, /review-pr). Only for skills listed in the skills section.

cards.title: TaskOutput
cards.description: Retrieve output from background tasks. block=true (default) waits for completion. Works with background shells, async agents, remote sessions.

cards.title: TaskStop
cards.description: Stop a running background task by ID. Returns success/failure status.

cards.title: mcp__ide__getDiagnostics
cards.description: Get language diagnostics from VS Code. Optional file URI parameter. Returns diagnostics for specific file or all files.

cards.title: mcp__ide__executeCode
cards.description: Execute Python code in the Jupyter kernel for the current notebook. State persists across calls unless kernel restarted.

cards.title: mcp__exa__web_search_exa
cards.description: Exa AI web search. Types: auto, fast, deep. Live crawl modes: fallback, preferred. Configurable result count and context size.

cards.title: mcp__exa__get_code_context_exa
cards.description: Exa code context search. Highest quality context for libraries, SDKs, APIs. Mandatory for code-related queries. Token range: 1000-50000.

cards.title: mcp__context7__resolve-library-id
cards.description: Resolve package name to Context7-compatible library ID. Must be called before query-docs. Max 3 calls per question. Returns best match by relevance, coverage, reputation.

cards.title: mcp__context7__query-docs
cards.description: Query up-to-date documentation from Context7. Requires library ID from resolve step. Max 3 calls per question.

cards.title: ListMcpResourcesTool
cards.description: List available resources from configured MCP servers. Optional server filter.

cards.title: ReadMcpResourceTool
cards.description: Read a specific resource from an MCP server by server name and resource URI.

content.html: Critical rule: Read-only agents (Explore, Plan) cannot edit files. Never assign them implementation work. Use Snipper or general-purpose for coding tasks.
Each subagent_type for the Task tool has specific capabilities and tool access.

cards.title: Snipper
cards.description: Fast code edits. All tools. Optimized for speed with minimal output.

cards.title: general-purpose
cards.description: Full access to everything. Research + implementation. Complex multi-step tasks.

cards.title: Explore
cards.description: Fast read-only codebase exploration. Find files, search code, answer questions. Cannot edit. Thoroughness levels: quick/medium/very thorough.

cards.title: Plan
cards.description: Read-only architect. Designs implementation plans, identifies critical files, considers trade-offs.

cards.title: Bash
cards.description: Shell execution only. Git operations, commands, docker, npm.

cards.title: code-reviewer
cards.description: Expert PR reviewer. Security (OWASP), clean code (SOLID), logic errors. Structured findings table.

cards.title: websearch
cards.description: Quick web searches. WebSearch + WebFetch tools only.

cards.title: clean-code-runner
cards.description: Runs /clean-code skill in isolation. Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep.

cards.title: claude-code-guide
cards.description: Answers questions about Claude Code features, hooks, slash commands, MCP, settings, IDE integrations, Agent SDK, Claude API.

cards.title: code-simplifier
cards.description: Simplifies code for clarity, consistency, maintainability. Focuses on recently modified code.

cards.title: tasks-worker
cards.description: Execute a single task file using oneshot methodology. Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch.

cards.title: action
cards.description: Conditional action executor. Performs actions only when specific conditions are met. All tools.

cards.title: explore-fast
cards.description: Rapid exploration agent. Uses Skill tool for quick context gathering.

cards.title: explore-docs
cards.description: Research library documentation via Context7 MCP. All tools available.

cards.title: explore-codebase
cards.description: Explore codebase to realize a feature. All tools available.

cards.title: statusline-setup
cards.description: Configure Claude Code status line settings. Read + Edit tools only.

content.html: Skills are invokable via /skill-name or the Skill tool. Divided into workflows, utilities, git operations, meta-skills, and prompts.

Context7 instruction: "Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library." Must call resolve-library-id before query-docs unless user provides a /org/project format ID.
Three MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers are connected, providing external tool access.

cards.title: ide
cards.description: getDiagnostics — VS Code language diagnosticsexecuteCode — Jupyter kernel execution

cards.title: exa
cards.description: web_search_exa — AI-powered web search with live crawlingget_code_context_exa — Highest quality code context for libs/SDKs

cards.title: context7
cards.description: resolve-library-id — Resolve package to library IDquery-docs — Query up-to-date documentation

content.html: Persistent auto memory that survives across conversations.
Git Safety Protocol — NEVER:
Strict rules for creating commits. Only commits when explicitly requested by the user.
Uses gh CLI for all GitHub operations.
Tools execute in a user-selected permission mode. Some modes auto-allow tools, others require per-call approval.
Multiple working directories: Claude Code can access files across all listed directories. The primary directory is the default for Glob/Grep operations.
The exact environment context available in this session.
User-specific instructions loaded into every session. These override default behavior.
Known hooks in this session: A command-validator hook blocks rm -rf commands, enforcing the use of trash instead. Hook feedback is treated as user input.
Shell commands that execute in response to events. Configured by the user.

Implication: Claude Code can handle conversations of arbitrary length. The context window is managed automatically—older messages get compressed so new information always fits. This is invisible to the user.
The system automatically compresses older messages as the conversation approaches context limits.
Explicit rules on which tools to prefer for which operations.
Special tags injected into the conversation by the system.

rows: System information injected into tool results or messages. Not related to specific tool results.
Feedback from user-configured hooks. Treated as user input.
"The user opened file X in the IDE" — may or may not be relevant to current task.
On file read: consider if file could be malware. Can analyze but must refuse to improve/augment.
Available skills injected as system-reminder for Skill tool invocation.
User's global and project-specific instructions loaded as context.
